players.name: Johny Placide
players.bio: Haiti's most-capped goalkeeper, Johny Placide is the veteran wall between the posts with 82 caps. Born in France, he's made Les Grenadiers his life's work. At 38, this World Cup is a remarkable final chapter.
players.facts: At 38, Placide is one of the oldest goalkeepers at WC2026 — older than some teammates' entire football careers.

players.name: Carlens Arcus
players.bio: Energetic right-back Carlens Arcus brings Ligue 2 know-how from Angers to the World Cup stage. Born in Port-au-Prince, he's a rare Haitian-born starter in European football. One international goal in 56 caps — a bonus from the engine room.
players.facts: Born in Port-au-Prince, Arcus is one of few Haiti regulars who actually grew up on the island he represents.

players.name: Keeto Thermoncy
players.bio: Teenage sensation Keeto Thermoncy arrives at WC2026 with just one cap but enormous potential. The 20-year-old Swiss-born defender plays for Young Boys in the Swiss Super League — big stage, big opportunity.
players.facts: Thermoncy is just 20 years old and has only 1 cap — making him one of the least experienced players at WC2026.

players.name: Ricardo Adé
players.bio: Experienced centre-back Ricardo Adé is a cornerstone of Haiti's defence with 59 caps. Born in Saint-Marc, he's plied his trade across South America with LDU Quito. Two international goals for a defender who reads the game well.
players.facts: Adé plays his club football in Ecuador with LDU Quito — one of very few Haitians in South American top-flight football.

players.name: Hannes Delcroix
players.bio: Swiss-born Hannes Delcroix brings European pedigree to Haiti's backline. The 27-year-old Lugano defender has only 7 caps but is a composed, ball-playing centre-back with an eye for the right pass.
players.birth_location: Petite Rivière de l Artibonite, Haiti
players.facts: Delcroix was born in Petite Rivière de l'Artibonite, Haiti, but developed his football entirely in Switzerland.

players.name: Carl Sainté
players.bio: Young defensive midfielder Carl Sainté brings USL Championship grit from El Paso to the World Cup. At just 23, the Grand-Goâve native is Haiti's terrier in midfield — all bite, no nonsense.
players.facts: Sainté plays in USL's El Paso Locomotive FC — one of the lower-profile clubs represented at WC2026.

players.name: Derrick Etienne Jr.
players.bio: Virginia-born winger Derrick Etienne Jr. has been a creative spark for Les Grenadiers for years. With 8 goals in 51 caps, he's a proven threat from the left. Now at Toronto FC, he brings MLS flair to the biggest stage.
players.facts: Etienne Jr. was born in Richmond, Virginia — one of several USA-born players choosing to represent Haiti.

players.name: Martin Expérience
players.bio: Left-back Martin Expérience brings a wonderfully apt name to a defensive role — and has the composure to match. The France-born 27-year-old represents Haiti with 21 caps and overlaps well from his Nancy base.
players.facts: His surname is literally 'Expérience' — and at 21 caps, he's quietly building plenty of it for Haiti.

players.name: Duckens Nazon
players.bio: Haiti's all-time top scorer, Duckens Nazon is a predator with 44 goals in 82 caps — a remarkable return. Born in France, his prolific nose for goal has taken him to Iran's Esteghlal. A genuine World Cup threat.
players.facts: Nazon's 44 international goals make him Haiti's all-time top scorer — a staggering record for a nation of this size.

players.name: Jean-Ricner Bellegarde
players.bio: Jean-Ricner Bellegarde is Haiti's marquee name — a dynamic central midfielder at Wolverhampton Wanderers in the Premier League. Still relatively new to international football with 10 caps, he brings elite-level quality to the No.10 role.
players.facts: Bellegarde is the only Haiti squad member playing in the English Premier League — their undisputed star man.

players.name: Louicius Deedson
players.bio: Pacey right winger Louicius Deedson has been prolific for Haiti with 10 goals in 32 caps — a fine return. Born in Tabarre, the FC Dallas forward brings MLS sharpness and direct running to Les Grenadiers' attack.
players.facts: Deedson was born in Tabarre, just outside Port-au-Prince — one of the few Haitian-born outfield stars at WC2026.

players.name: Alexandre Pierre
players.bio: Alexandre Pierre is the dependable backup between the sticks — a young Sochaux goalkeeper with 16 caps already under his belt. Born in Aubervilliers, France, he's the reliable understudy with aspirations to step up.
players.facts: Pierre plays for Sochaux, one of France's most historic clubs — though currently plying their trade outside Ligue 1.

players.name: Duke Lacroix
players.bio: New Jersey-born Duke Lacroix is a marauding left-back with three international goals in 16 caps — impressive for a defender. The Colorado Springs Switchbacks man brings USL experience and an attacking instinct to his role.
players.birth_location: New Egypt, New Jersey, United States
players.facts: Lacroix was born in New Egypt, New Jersey — a real place, and arguably the best town name in this entire squad.

players.name: Garven Metusala
players.bio: Montreal-born centre-back Garven Metusala is a solid presence in Haiti's defence with 16 caps. The Colorado Springs Switchbacks defender is one of several Canadian-born players flying the Haitian flag at WC2026.
players.facts: Metusala was born on New Year's Eve 1999 — technically a millennial and a Gen Z player simultaneously.

players.name: Ruben Providence
players.bio: Ruben Providence is a lively left winger with three goals in 15 caps for Les Grenadiers. The France-born Almere City forward brings Dutch Eredivisie flair to Haiti's wide areas — quick, direct, and dangerous.
players.facts: Providence plays in the Dutch Eredivisie with Almere City — one of the Netherlands' newest top-flight clubs.

players.name: Lenny Joseph
players.bio: Paris-born striker Lenny Joseph has made a stunning start to international football — one goal in just two caps. The Ferencváros forward brings Hungarian top-flight pedigree and predatory instincts to Haiti's frontline.
players.facts: Joseph has scored 1 goal in just 2 caps — a 50% conversion rate that even Duckens Nazon can't match.

players.name: Danley Jean Jacques
players.bio: Danley Jean Jacques is a dynamic box-to-box defensive midfielder with a remarkable six international goals from deep. Born in Petit-Goâve, the Philadelphia Union man is energetic, combative, and occasionally ruthless in front of goal.
players.facts: Six goals in 31 caps from defensive midfield — Jean Jacques scores more than most forwards at this tournament.

players.name: Wilson Isidor
players.bio: Wilson Isidor is one of Haiti's most exciting forwards — two goals in just four caps signal a player ready to explode. The Rennes-born Sunderland striker brings Championship quality and pace to Les Grenadiers' attack.
players.facts: Isidor plays for Sunderland in the English Championship — a club with a fanbase that will definitely adopt Haiti as a second team.

players.name: Yassin Fortuné
players.bio: Yassin Fortuné offers Haiti a powerful centre-forward option from Vizela in Portugal. The Aubervilliers-born striker has four caps and is still seeking his first international goal — WC2026 could be his breakthrough moment.
players.facts: Fortuné plays in Portugal's Primeira Liga with Vizela — his name literally means 'luck' in French. Convenient.

players.name: Frantzdy Pierrot
players.bio: Frantzdy Pierrot is Haiti's prolific second striker with a stunning 34 goals in 51 caps. Born in Cap-Haïtien, the Çaykur Rizespor forward is a goal machine with a World Cup to add to his collection.
players.facts: Pierrot averages nearly a goal every 1.5 games for Haiti — one of the best international strike rates in CONCACAF.

players.name: Josué Casimir
players.bio: Guadeloupe-born right-back Josué Casimir brings Ligue 1 quality from Auxerre to the World Cup. With 7 caps, the 24-year-old is building his international reputation — a technically gifted full-back with room to grow.
players.facts: Casimir was born in Baie-Mahault, Guadeloupe — making him one of the few Caribbean island-born players in the squad.

players.name: Jean-Kévin Duverne
players.bio: Paris-born Jean-Kévin Duverne is a commanding centre-back with 17 caps and a scorer's instinct — one international goal already. The Gent defender brings Belgian Pro League grit and aerial dominance to Haiti's backline.
players.facts: Duverne plays for Gent in Belgium — a club that has regularly featured in European competition in recent seasons.

players.name: Josué Duverger
players.bio: Montreal-born goalkeeper Josué Duverger is Haiti's third-choice stopper with seven caps. Playing for Cosmos Koblenz in Germany, the 26-year-old is a well-travelled custodian gaining experience across European football.
players.facts: Duverger plays for Cosmos Koblenz — named after the legendary New York Cosmos. Football's most ambitious tribute act.

players.name: Wilguens Paugain
players.bio: Right-back Wilguens Paugain plays his club football for Zulte Waregem in Belgium, bringing European experience to Haiti's defensive options. The 24-year-old has eight caps and is developing into a reliable squad option.
players.facts: Paugain's birthplace is listed as Thomazeau, France — though Thomazeau is actually a commune in Haiti. A real puzzle.

players.name: Dominique Simon
players.bio: Pontoise-born defensive midfielder Dominique Simon is a no-frills, hard-working presence in central midfield. With just two caps, he's a squad player for Les Grenadiers at WC2026 — but every great story starts somewhere.
players.facts: Simon plays for Tatran Prešov in Slovakia — one of the most geographically unexpected clubs represented at WC2026.

players.name: Woodensky Pierre
players.bio: Born in Cité Soleil, one of Haiti's most deprived neighbourhoods, Woodensky Pierre's journey to the World Cup is the stuff of dreams. The 21-year-old Violette midfielder has just one cap — but what a stage to earn more.
players.facts: Pierre plays for Violette AC in the Haitian league — one of the very few homegrown Haitian-based players at WC2026.
